title: FISMA Reporting Automation

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-generated from legacy markdown 'Regulations' line during the per-UC migration. Requires SME review to fill in version, clause, mode, and assurance for FISMA.

spl:
index=notable OR index=itsm sourcetype IN ("notable:incident","jira:fisma")
| eval fisma_system=coalesce(system_id, bureausystem)
| stats count(eval(severity="critical")) as crit, count as total, avg(days_open) as avg_age by fisma_system
| where crit>0 OR avg_age>30
| sort -crit
description: Aggregates incident, POA&M, and control metrics for annual FISMA/CyberScope submissions.
value: Aggregates incident, POA&M, and control metrics for annual FISMA/CyberScope submissions.
implementation: Align with NIST RMF system inventory; automate CSV for OMB dashboards; CIO attestation workflow.
visualization: Table (system risk), Bar chart (POA&M age), Single value (contained incidents %).
knownFalsePositives: FISMA reporting noise when ticket systems, evidence indexes, and POA&Ms are mid-migration; align reporting periods before treating as a new material weakness.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ES, ticketing.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=notable`, `sourcetype="jira:fisma"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Align with NIST RMF system inventory; automate CSV for OMB dashboards; CIO attestation workflow.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=notable OR index=itsm sourcetype IN ("notable:incident","jira:fisma")
| eval fisma_system=coalesce(system_id, bureausystem)
| stats count(eval(severity="critical")) as crit, count as total, avg(days_open) as avg_age by fisma_system
| where crit>0 OR avg_age>30
| sort -crit
```

Understanding this SPL

**FISMA Reporting Automation** — Aggregates incident, POA&M, and control metrics for annual FISMA/CyberScope submissions.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=notable`, `sourcetype="jira:fisma"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ES, ticketing. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: notable, itsm.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=notable, index=itsm. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fisma_system** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by fisma_system** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where crit>0 OR avg_age>30` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (system risk), Bar chart (POA&M age), Single value (contained incidents %).
grandmaExplanation: In our line of business, we pay close attention to "FISMA Reporting Automation" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
